article_title: Autonomous Ships Cross Two Milestones in a Single Month as China Completes First Unmanned Port Call and U.S. Navy Begins Building Crewless Warships

content_preview.title: Autonomous Ships Cross Two Milestones in a Single Month as China Completes First Unmanned Port Call and U.S. Navy Begins Building Crewless Warships
content_preview.summary: China's Zhi Fei completed a fully autonomous port call at Qingdao while the U.S. Navy's Liberty Class entered construction, as the IMO races to finalize global rules by 2030.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Autonomous shipping reached two distinct milestones in February and March 2026, signaling that crewless vessels are moving from experimental demonstrations to operational reality on both commercial and military fronts. In China, the smart container ship Zhi Fei completed the world's first fully unmanned port call at Qingdao, executing every step from navigation to cargo handling without a single human on board. Days later in Louisiana, construction began on the U.S. Navy's Liberty C...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured dual-story format covering two genuinely newsworthy milestones. The article is organized logically: Zhi Fei port call, Liberty Class construction start, IMO regulatory context, and a candid 'What We Don't Know' section that demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility. The 981-word count falls within the News category range (400-1200). Transitions between the two main subjects are smooth, and the IMO section provides necessary regulatory context without becoming a digression.
editor_notes.source_verification: Seven sources cited, none on the blocklist. Splash247, CGTN, Container News, New Atlas, PR Newswire, Military.com, and IMO.org are all credible outlets in their respective domains. A date discrepancy exists between sources: the article states 'February 21' for the Zhi Fei port call (matching the CGTN article URL and its reported date), while Splash247 reports February 25. The CGTN source explicitly confirms February 21, so the article's stated date is supported by its primary Chinese state-media source. The discrepancy between sources is noted but does not constitute an error in the article. IMO.org is an authoritative primary source for regulatory claims.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Key technical claims verified against sources: 300-TEU capacity, battery-electric propulsion, vacuum mooring system under 30 seconds, A-TOS and A-ECS systems, approximately 1,300 voyages since 2022, Qingdao-Dongjiakou-Rizhao routes — all confirmed. Liberty Class: 190 feet, Conrad Shipyard Louisiana, over 10,000 nautical mile range, over 150 metric tons payload, Damen Stan Patrol 6009 hull with axe bow, 10-20 vessels/year production target — all confirmed by PR Newswire. The 25-knot top speed and 770-tonne displacement figures appear in the body but were not independently verified against retrieved source text; however no contradicting information was found. The IMO 2030 binding framework and 2026 non-mandatory code adoption target are consistent with the IMO source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Tone is neutral and appropriately measured. The article avoids sensationalism and does not editorialize. The 'What We Don't Know' section is particularly commendable — it explicitly limits scope claims, acknowledges that the Zhi Fei operates on short-haul controlled routes, questions the scalability to large container ships, and flags open questions about Liberty Class deployment timelines. No AI self-reference. No loaded language.
editor_notes.originality: No existing coverage of this topic in the archive. The dual-milestone framing is original and editorially justified — both events occurred in the same month and together represent a genuine inflection point in autonomous maritime development. The parallel civilian/military structure provides clear thematic coherence.
concerns: Minor date ambiguity: article states 'February 21' for the Zhi Fei port call, sourced to CGTN, while Splash247 (also cited) reports February 25. The CGTN source supports the article's stated date, so this is not an error, but it reflects real-world reporting inconsistency across sources.

The 25-knot speed and 770-tonne displacement figures for the Liberty Class were not directly confirmed in retrieved source text. These details were not contradicted, but their primary source is the PR Newswire release which should contain them.
recommendations: Consider adding splash247.com, newatlas.com, military.com, and container-news.com to the source allowlist — all are established trade and specialist publications appropriate for maritime and defense coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: A high-quality News piece covering two distinct but thematically linked milestones. Claims are well-sourced and verified, structure is clear, tone is neutral, and the article appropriately distinguishes confirmed facts from open questions. The source-allowlist warning is a configuration gap, not a quality issue. Recommended for approval.
